mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in unemployment rate and increase in payrolls signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and investor confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 100k occurs but 20-day returns are negative in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production index indicates robust manufacturing activity, which correlates with stronger corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases by > 2% MoM but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 35% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in housing starts signals reduced consumer demand for durable goods, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST declines by > 5% MoM but 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% erodes real returns, prompting central bank rate hikes that depress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% but 20-day returns are positive in > 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in real estate financing applications indicates elevated consumer confidence and spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS increases by > 3% MoM but 5-day returns are negative in > 30% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A labor market overheating scenario, defined by unemployment below 4.5% and wage growth above 3%, leads to inflationary pressures and negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS > 150k occurs but 20-day returns are positive in > 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 2.5%) and low policy rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) creates a 'stagflation' risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and FEDFUNDS < 1% occurs but 20-day returns are positive in > 35% of cases.
